text: "Honestly, the big things for me are clarity and structure — I want headers that actually organize the content, not just label it. I write for engineers who are smart but haven't touched this particular system before, so I try not to over-explain fundamentals while still giving enough context that nothing feels like it came out of nowhere. And wherever it makes sense, I'll include code examples, because there's only so much you can communicate in prose when you're talking about how something actually works."
